title: eutrochium fistulosum
intro: Eutrochium fistulosum, also known as Joe-Pye weed, is a perennial wildflower native to eastern North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to aid in the expulsion of mucus and to reduce fever. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia, and its roots have been used to treat digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Eutrochium fistulosum can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote sweating and to relieve symptoms of colds and flu.
